name: Antidoc capability VIPM dependencies
description: OPT-IN, capability-scoped VIPM dependencies for the Antidoc documentation generator. This is the source manifest for antidoc.vipc (generated by build-tooling-vipc.py, same format as ci-tooling.packages.json). It is deliberately kept OUT of ci-tooling.vipc (the core set every action runner needs) so the Antidoc add-on and its dependency chain are baked into the worker image ONLY when the Antidoc capability is installed - the image build stages this VIPC only when the run-antidoc workflow is present. This is the pattern for every new VIPM-installed capability: give it its own .github/labview/<capability>/<capability>.vipc and bake it only if/when the functionality is wanted. Edit this list + regenerate (python3 .github/labview/vipm/build-tooling-vipc.py --manifest .github/labview/antidoc/antidoc.packages.json --out .github/labview/antidoc/antidoc.vipc --defaults none).

target.name: LabVIEW
target.comment: labviewVersion must match the LabVIEW in the NI base image. The generator maps it to the .vipc Target <Version> (2026 -> '26.1 (64-bit)').

vipmPackages.comment: Top-level packages only. Each is emitted as a <Package Included=false> entry, so `vipm apply_vipc` downloads it (and its dependencies, listed here for reference only) from the configured VIPM repository at build time.

packages.name: wovalab_lib_antidoc_cli
packages.summary: Antidoc CLI by Wovalab - generate LabVIEW project documentation headlessly (the supported CI/CD path).
packages.note: Doc generation renders diagrams via a Kroki server at run time (Wovalab-hosted by default); only the doc-gen step needs that network, not the install.
